profile.name: Robert
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; tends to take the lead on investments and big-picture planning, but wants decisions to feel jointly agreed
profile.financial_literacy_level: High—comfortable with investment concepts, account types, and trade-offs; tends to remember exact figures and will correct small inaccuracies
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate in practice: prefers a plan that protects downside and avoids big surprises, but is willing to take measured risk when the rationale is clear and tied to goals like capital preservation, education, and estate planning
profile.communication_style: Direct and structured; often answers with specific numbers, then asks a clarifying question. Uses occasional hedges like “roughly” or “if I’m remembering right,” but generally has strong recall and will pause to verify details
emotional_tendencies: gets uneasy when plans feel vague
relieved by clear timelines and written next steps
profile.decision_making_style: Evidence-driven: wants options compared side-by-side (tax impact, risk, liquidity), then decides once assumptions are explicit
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open—will trust quickly if the advisor demonstrates competence, transparency on fees, and follows through on action items
internal_biases: anchoring to known balances and past performance
overconfidence in areas he knows well (numbers/investing)
hidden_concerns: wants to ensure the household’s higher tax bracket (35%) is handled efficiently without creating complexity
quietly worries about being “inactive” employment-wise and how that looks in long-term planning

profile.name: Emily
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; focuses on day-to-day cash flow and lifestyle stability, and acts as a check on whether recommendations feel practical
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate—understands budgeting, retirement basics, and the purpose of insurance, but may mix up terms (premium vs. benefit, balance vs. payment) and prefers plain-English explanations
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but more loss-averse than Robert: tends to worry about market drops and prefers smoother outcomes, even if it means slightly lower upside; asks “what happens if…” questions
profile.communication_style: Conversational and values-based; speaks in round numbers and approximations, sometimes says “I think” or “maybe.” Will interrupt briefly to clarify how something affects monthly expenses or the mortgage payment
emotional_tendencies: stress spikes when monthly obligations feel tight
reassured by contingency plans and emergency buffers
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-oriented: wants to hear the recommendation, then talk it through together; prefers a short list of choices rather than many permutations
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-guarded at first—trust builds through patience, clear explanations, and not talking down; appreciates being asked for her priorities directly
internal_biases: availability bias (recent headlines influence perceived risk)
hidden_concerns: concerned about health being only “fair” and how that could affect retirement timing and insurance decisions
worries that large fixed costs (like the mortgage payment) reduce flexibility if income changes
